heading: Postage collected unlawfully
label: 1726 Postage collected unlawfully
text: Whoever, being a postmaster or other person authorized to receive the postage of mail matter, fraudulently demands or receives any rate of postage or gratuity or reward other than is provided by law for the postage of such mail matter, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than six months, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 330 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 20735 Stat. 1128Minor verbal changes were made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $100”.Pub. L. 103–322
